task_completion.description: Locate and select the 'First Amendment Jurisprudence' collection
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'First Amendment Jurisprudence' collection is visible and highlighted/selected in the left sidebar panel of the Juris-M application.
A right-click context menu is open on the 'First Amendment Jurisprudence' collection with 'Export Collection...' selected, or the Export dialog window is open.
The Export dialog window is visible, and the 'Format' dropdown menu is explicitly set to 'BibTeX'.

task_completion.description: Configure the save directory and filename
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Save file dialog is open. The current directory is set to 'Documents' (or /home/ga/Documents) and the filename field contains 'first_amendment_refs.bib'.

The Save button is clicked in the file dialog, the dialog disappears returning to the main Juris-M window, or a terminal/file manager confirms the existence of 'first_amendment_refs.bib' in the Documents folder.

integrity.description: The agent used the Juris-M GUI to perform the export
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Juris-M application windows, context menus, and export dialogs, rather than terminal commands or scripts being used to generate the file.

integrity.description: The agent exported the specific collection, not the entire library
integrity.visual_evidence: The right-click menu was opened specifically on the 'First Amendment Jurisprudence' collection, not on 'My Library' or any other collection.

integrity.description: The BibTeX file was generated by the software, not manually typed
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of a text editor being used to manually type, copy, or paste BibTeX entries for the Sullivan and Tinker cases.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires exporting ONLY the 'First Amendment Jurisprudence' collection (which contains exactly 2 cases: New York Times Co. v. Sullivan and Tinker v. Des Moines) to BibTeX format. The full library contains 10 items. The expected output file must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/first_amendment_refs.bib. Verify that the agent exports the specific collection rather than 'My Library', and uses the Juris-M GUI to select the BibTeX format and save the file.
